sections.paragraph: f. wreathen work, like a rope twisted and wreathed; see the root Kal No. 1. Exo 28:22, “and thou shalt make for the breastplate, גַּבְלֻת מַעֲשֵׂה עֲבֹת זָהָב טָהֹור שַׁרְשֹׁות wreathen chains, with twisted work of pure gold.” Well rendered by the LXX. κροσσοὺς συμπεπλεγμένους, Exo 39:15. As to the same thing, Exo 28:14, “and two chains of pure gold, מִגְבָּלֹות תַּעֲשֶׂה אֹתָם מַעֲשֶׂה עֲבֹת wreathen thou shalt make them, of twisted work.” LXX. καταμεμιγμένα (ἐν ἄνθεσι). If I understand this, small chains are meant, made of double threads of gold, twisted like a rope, fchnurenförmige Kettchen, Goldfchnur, and מַעֲשֵׂה עֲבֹת specifies it more accurately, by epexegesis.
